Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Pltfs' Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-11-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS JAMILEH BAJJALIEH, ANTON BAJJALIEH Motion to Strike Portions of Plaintiff's Second Amended Complaint. Appearances: Jody Struck, Esq. of Haapla, Thompson & Abern, LLP (510) 763-2324 appearing for Defendant and Moving party; Emmanuel Enyinwa, Attorney at Law, 956-6100 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party. (This tentative ruling on this motion is not being contested.) The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to strike granted without leave to amend as to punitive damages against defendant Jamileh Bajjalieh, whose name will be stricken from the second amended complaint at page 10, line 7. No reasonable likelihood defect can be cured by amendment. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-11-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS JAMILEH BAJJALIEH, ANTON BAJJALIEH DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Jody Struck, Esq. of Haapla, Thompson & Abern, LLP (510) 763-2324 appearing for Defendant and Moving party; Emmanuel Enyinwa, Attorney at Law, 956-6100 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer overruled, ten days leave to answer. Factual issues exist whether defendant exercised due care when she took over the 911 call and refused to let paramedics in, whether she increased the risk of harm to decedent, or whether any harm was suffered due to decedent's reliance on defendant's undertaking. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS JAMILEH BAJJALIEH AND ANTON BAJJALIEH'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Emmanuel Enyinwa, Attorney at Law, (415) 956-6100 appearing for Plaintiffs and opposing party; Jody Struck, Esq. of Haapala, Altura, Thompson & Abern LLP (510) 763-2324, appearing for defendants and moving party. (The Tentative ruling is not being contested.) The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION.
